sanskrit: तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा। अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा॥
transliteration: tatraika-sthaṃ jagat-kṛtsnaṃ pravibhaktam-anekadhā | apaśyad-deva-devasya śarīre pāṇḍavas-tadā ||
literal_meaning: There, in one place, the entire universe—though split into many divisions—Arjuna saw within the body of the God-of-gods.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s ego-boundary dissolves; he perceives the whole cosmos as a single, living gestalt inside Krishna. The experience is a non-dual vision that collapses inner/outer, self/other, and time/space into one luminous field.
psychological_state: Peak transpersonal experience: simultaneous expansion of consciousness and shattering of personal identity; akin to a controlled psychedelic breakthrough or near-death experience.
trigger_scenario: A paramedic on a 48-hour shift glimpses the ER as one living organism—every patient, monitor, and heartbeat felt as her own body—then snaps back, forever changed.
anchor_text: See the whole universe as one living presence within you.
